content.text: I have several configuration settings provided as keyword arguments, and I need to apply them to an instance of a class while ensuring that certain single string values are automatically converted into lists.
What happens if a user passes an empty list or None for a key even though it is required to be a list?
If the user passes `None`, the initial check `if v is not None:` will skip setting the attribute entirely. If they pass an empty list, it will be assigned correctly as lists are not strings.
